_comment: Branch protection for `main`, checked in so it is reviewable and restorable.
Only checks that run on EVERY pull request may be listed in contexts. ci-dotnet, ci-frontend
and ci-python are path-filtered: on a docs-only PR they never start, so their check context
never reports, and a PR requiring them would block forever waiting on a job that will never
run. They still gate every PR that touches their paths — they just cannot be required here.
required_pull_request_reviews is null on purpose. GitHub does not count an App's review
(CodeRabbit's) toward the approving-review quota, and nobody can approve their own PR, so
requiring one approval on a solo-maintained repo deadlocks every PR.
enforce_admins is true: the gate applies to the owner too, otherwise it is just advice.
If CodeRabbit is down, main is frozen — see docs/code-review.md for the escape hatch.
